description: Slices of bread soaked in a beaten egg and milk mixture, then pan-cooked until golden. It is commonly served sweet, though savory versions also exist.
preparation_steps: Whisk eggs with milk and optional flavorings.
Dip bread briefly so it absorbs the custard without falling apart.
Cook on a buttered pan or griddle until golden on both sides.

health_advice.benefits: Provides protein from eggs and can be made more balanced with fruit and moderate toppings.
health_advice.risks: Sugar, syrup, cream, and thick enriched breads can raise calories quickly.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use whole-grain bread and top with fruit rather than excessive syrup.

dietary_notes.note: Usually vegetarian. Vegan versions need egg and dairy substitutes, and gluten-free versions need gluten-free bread.

best_served_with: Bacon or sausage for non-vegetarian breakfasts

popular_regions: France in older forms such as pain perdu
